## SECTION 1 — Global Pulse (Geopolitics & World Economy)
Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East persist, with a mourner at the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei calling for the death of Donald Trump, amidst a broader U.S.-Iran conflict that has impacted oil supply routes. ## SECTION 2 — Global News (Complete Continental Coverage)
### Americas
A dangerous heatwave is intensifying across New York City and the broader East Coast, bringing scorching temperatures, high humidity, and oppressive summer conditions through July 5-6, 2026. Cities like Newark, New Jersey, recorded new all-time highs of 105°F (41°C), straining power grids and prompting officials to urge residents to stay indoors. Meanwhile, the Midwest is experiencing a strong and unstable weather pattern, with a high risk of severe thunderstorms, heavy rainfall, and gusty winds in areas like Chicago and surrounding states.
### Europe
Southern Europe is battling widespread wildfires, with hundreds of firefighters across Portugal, Spain, France, and Greece responding to infernos that have devastated over 19,000 hectares of land. Temperatures are expected to reach 40°C in some areas, exacerbating the blazes and forcing evacuations. The EU is intensifying efforts to resolve issues with its new Entry/Exit System (EES) after airports and airlines reported wait times of up to five hours, warning of potential summer travel chaos.
### Asia-Pacific
Sri Lanka is actively seeking support from the Asia-Pacific region for an integrated climate action program, focusing on sustainable development and leveraging blue carbon ecosystems. The Aurora Pacific Economic Zone and Freeport Authority (APECO) in the Philippines is engaging Malaysian investors for clean energy and airport development projects, positioning itself as a strategic Pacific-facing gateway for trade. RoboCup 2026, a major robotics and AI competition, is taking place in Incheon, South Korea, bringing together over 3,000 participants and 15,000 visitors.

RoboCup 2026, often referred to as the "World Cup of Robotics," is underway in Incheon, South Korea, showcasing advancements in robotics and artificial intelligence from June 30 to July 6, 2026. The EU is increasing efforts to address issues with its new Entry/Exit System (EES) at borders, which has led to wait times of up to five hours, following warnings from the European aviation industry. This system records travelers' personal and biometric data to track overstays and refusals of entry in EU nations, excluding Ireland and Cyprus, and other Schengen Area countries. ## SECTION 13 — India National (Politics & Governance)
The Defence Acquisition Council, chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, approved defence acquisition proposals worth nearly ₹52,000 crore to boost the operational capabilities of the Army, Navy, and Air Force. Leaders of the INDIA bloc addressed a joint memorandum to Chief Justice of India Justice Surya Kant, alleging election manipulation, partisan conduct by the Election Commission, and misuse of central investigative agencies against opposition parties. The Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) called for Hindu unity and demanded strict action against those involved in the alleged theft from the Ayodhya Ram Temple's donation boxes. ISRO is preparing for the next Gaganyaan Mission Test, a significant step towards India's first human spaceflight program. India's Semiconductor Mission is gaining momentum, with new chip manufacturing projects nearing commercial production. DRDO successfully tested advanced hypersonic technology, aiming to strengthen India's next-generation defense capabilities. Indian scientists developed a new AI-based healthcare system to improve the early detection of critical diseases. The government expanded the Digital India Initiative with new AI and cybersecurity projects to boost innovation and digital infrastructure. The Centre ordered the removal of three Chinese apps—BAT-BMS, Lossigy, and Epoch-i-ion—due to concerns about their misuse to remotely disable e-rickshaws. Heavy rainfall flooded parts of Mumbai, causing slow road traffic and train delays, prompting an IMD orange alert.

## SECTION 14 — Telangana State Updates
Telangana Excise Minister Jupally Krishna Rao engaged in a public debate on the state's finances, challenging the previous Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) government over outstanding liabilities, which he claimed increased from ₹90,161 crore in June 2014 to ₹8,21,651 crore by December 1, 2023. Deputy Chief Minister Bhatti Vikramarka reiterated these accusations, stating the previous BRS government pushed Telangana into an ₹8.21 lakh crore debt trap and challenged former Chief Minister K. Chandrasekhar Rao to a public debate. The Telangana Cabinet approved the first phase of the Musi River Rejuvenation Project, allocating an estimated cost of ₹7,345.12 crore for a 21-kilometer stretch. The Telangana government decided to challenge the new rural employment scheme, VB-G RAM G, in the Supreme Court to safeguard the state's rights, despite deciding to implement it. The government clarified that claims of 40 lakh tonnes of unaccounted coal from Singareni Collieries Company Limited (SCCL) are untrue and directed the SCCL Vigilance Director to conduct a full-scale inquiry into the matter. Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka questioned why the previous government failed to secure new coal blocks for Singareni. The cabinet also approved providing mid-day meals, snacks, and milk to students up to class 12, including teachers and staff. Construction of Telangana Institute of Medical Sciences (TIMS) super-specialty hospitals in Sanatnagar, LB Nagar, Alwal, and a state-run super-specialty hospital in Warangal received approval for accelerated completion. The recruitment of 6,278 various posts in these hospitals was also approved. The Meteorological Centre forecasted heavy to very heavy rainfall at isolated places across several Telangana districts over the next 48 hours, with thunderstorms and lightning expected for three days. Hyderabad district administration established 16 help desks at Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) offices for the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process.

---

## SECTION 15 — Andhra Pradesh State Updates
Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu launched the construction of the JSW Rayalaseema Steel Plant in Kadapa district, a project with an investment of ₹16,350 crores. He also virtually inaugurated the Jindal Industrial Park in Vizianagaram district. The Centre formed a high-level expert committee under the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) to address the declining prices of Totapuri mangoes in Andhra Pradesh and recommend measures to support farmers. The Central Government approved the appointment of three judicial officers—Sunitha Gandham, Alapati Giridhar, and Purushottam Kumar Chintelapudi—as additional judges of the Andhra Pradesh High Court. CM Naidu wrapped up a week-long district tour, which included launching works for JSW's Rayalaseema Steel Plant and JSW Neo Energy solar captive power project (totaling over ₹20,000 crore investment). During his tour, Naidu launched over 30 projects worth ₹9,322 crore under the Swarna Kuppam initiative, including Adidas footwear manufacturing and aircraft training-related projects, expected to create over 87,000 jobs. On July 2, CM Naidu, Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan, and Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan launched the Centre's VB-G RAM G mission from Kodur, involving ₹7,700 crore in central funds and ₹4,000 crore from the state. Andhra Pradesh Minister Nara Lokesh announced that the state government, in collaboration with JSW Group, will establish a world-class Sports Centre of Excellence to enhance the sports ecosystem and nurture international athletes. The government released ₹2.23 crore for the training of 18 athletes selected for this year's Asian Games, Commonwealth Games, and Para Commonwealth Games. Four people died on the spot after a speeding truck collided with a passenger auto in Prakasam, Andhra Pradesh.

## SECTION 18 — Deep Analysis of Most Important Breaking News
**What Happened:** At the funeral of the slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on Sunday, a mourner publicly called for the death of Donald Trump in front of hundreds of thousands of attendees. This incident occurred amidst heightened tensions stemming from the U.S.-Iran conflict, which has seen disruptions to oil supply routes and mutual warnings of resumed military action.
**Root Causes:** The call for Trump's death is rooted in the deep-seated animosity and geopolitical friction between Iran and the United States, exacerbated by recent military confrontations and the ongoing impact on regional stability and oil markets. The funeral served as a platform for expressing strong anti-American sentiment within a highly charged atmosphere of national mourning and calls for vengeance.
**Historical Background:** The relationship between Iran and the U.S. has been fraught with tension for decades, marked by sanctions, proxy conflicts, and periods of direct confrontation. The recent U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, which temporarily disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, escalated these long-standing hostilities, creating a volatile environment where such public declarations are made.
**Key Actors:** Key actors include the Iranian leadership and public expressing anti-U.S. sentiment, former U.S. President Donald Trump, and various international mediators attempting to de-escalate regional tensions and secure a lasting ceasefire in related conflicts like the one in Gaza.
**Strategic Importance:** This incident underscores the extreme polarization and potential for further escalation in the Middle East. Such public statements can fuel radical elements, complicate diplomatic efforts, and maintain a high level of alert for security agencies globally, particularly concerning U.S. interests and personnel.
**Possible Long-Term Consequences:** Long-term consequences could include a perpetuation of hostile rhetoric, increased difficulty in diplomatic resolutions between Iran and Western powers, and a continued risk of regional destabilization. It may also solidify hardline positions within Iran, affecting its foreign policy and internal political dynamics.

## SECTION 19 — This Day in History (Global)
*   In 1483, King Richard III of England was crowned.
*   In 1777, British forces captured Fort Ticonderoga during the American Revolution.
*   In 1858, Lyman Blake patented the shoe manufacturing machine.
*   In 1885, Louis Pasteur successfully tested his anti-rabies vaccine.
*   In 1905, fingerprints were exchanged for the first time between officials in Europe and the U.S. for John Walker.
*   In 1917, Arab forces led by T.E. Lawrence captured the port of Aqaba from the Turks during World War I.
*   In 1919, a British dirigible completed the first crossing of the Atlantic Ocean by an airship, landing in New York.
*   In 1923, the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) was established.
*   In 1942, Anne Frank and her family took refuge from the Nazis in Amsterdam.
*   In 1945, U.S. President Truman signed an order creating the Medal of Freedom.
*   In 1957, American tennis player Althea Gibson became the first Black player to win the women's singles title at Wimbledon.
*   In 1964, Malawi gained its independence from Britain.
*   In 1975, Comoros became independent after 137 years of French rule.
*   In 2013, Asiana Airlines Flight 214 crash-landed at San Francisco International Airport, killing 3 people and injuring at least 190 others.

---

## SECTION 20 — This Day in History (India)
*   In 1837, Ramakrishna Gopal Bhandarkar, an Indian scholar, orientalist, and social reformer, was born.
*   In 1890, Dhan Gopal Mukerji, the first successful Indian man of letters in the United States and Newbery Medal winner, was born.
*   In 1892, Dadabhai Naoroji became the first Indian to be elected as a Member of Parliament in Britain.
*   In 1901, Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee, founder of the Bharatiya Jana Sangha, was born in Kolkata.
*   In 1944, Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose first referred to Mahatma Gandhi as the Father of the Nation.
*   In 1956, Anil Madhav Dave, an Indian environmentalist and politician known for Narmada conservation, was born.
*   In 1986, Jagjivan Ram, a prominent Indian politician and long-time spokesman for the Dalits, passed away.
*   In 2002, Dhirubhai Ambani, the Indian business tycoon and founder of Reliance Industries Limited, passed away in Mumbai.
*   In 2006, Nathula Pass, a trading post between India and China, reopened for business after being closed since the Sino-Indian war of 1962.

The past week has seen significant global developments across various sectors. Geopolitically, the U.S.-Iran conflict remains a critical point, influencing oil prices and regional stability. ## SECTION 25 — Strategic Insight / Underreported Development
An underreported development is Australia's escalating "Asia Knowledge Crisis," as highlighted by a new parliamentary inquiry. The report, "Security and Prosperity in Asia: Building Australia's Asia Capability," reveals a significant decline in participation in Asian languages and Asian Studies to its lowest level since 1965. This erosion of capability, despite deepening economic, diplomatic, and educational ties with Asia, is now identified as a national vulnerability, potentially hindering Australia's ability to engage meaningfully with the region.
